title: Echinopterys eglandulosa
taxonomy: Echinopterys eglandulosa is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant is a member of the order Gentianales. The family Rubiaceae is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, containing over 600 genera and 13,000 species. Echinopterys eglandulosa is also a member of the genus Echinopterys, which is a monotypic genus, meaning it only contains a single species. The classification of Echinopterys eglandulosa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family Rubiaceae.

intro: Echinopterys eglandulosa, commonly known as Kalmegh or Kalonji, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The health benefits of Echinopterys eglandulosa include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been reported to aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
